opps.title: data.CMS.gov — Development and Operations of CMS Open Data Platform
opps.agency: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), Office of Enterprise Data and Analytics (OEDA)
opps.description: CMS OEDA seeks contractor to develop, operate, and maintain data.cms.gov — the authoritative open data platform hosting 170+ datasets for Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and Marketplace. Scope: agile product management, human-centered design (HCD) and user research, Drupal CMS development and operations, DevSecOps, API management, help desk operations, data operations (dataset publishing, QA), ATO maintenance, Section 508/WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, and optional hosting/CDN migration and data website consolidation. ~15 FTE team. FFP + T&M CLINs. US-based personnel only. 99.5% uptime SLA.
opps.summary: GSA MRAS. CMS open data platform — 170+ datasets, Drupal, HCD/UX, DevSecOps, API, help desk. Estimated M-M.

deep_analysis.what_they_need_deep: CMS OEDA needs an expert partner to keep data.CMS.gov running as the authoritative, always-on open data platform for Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and Marketplace data. The core problem is ensuring the platform is reliable, secure, accessible, and user-friendly for a massive, diverse audience — while modernizing the tech stack (Drupal), supporting data operations, and handling compliance and uptime at an enterprise scale.
deep_analysis.strategic_take: BLN24 is a confirmed federal Drupal prime (MBDA.gov, $4.8M) with direct experience running a multi-tenant, high-traffic, Section 508-compliant Drupal platform for the Department of Commerce. Our CMS MNPS ($16M) work at CMS shows we can handle mission-critical, regulated content at scale for this agency, including complex stakeholder management and 508 compliance. Our IRS BPA ($5.8M) and CBP digital work prove deep HCD/UX research chops. However, our DevSecOps and ATO experience is lighter — strongest in AEM (Census DWS III, $8.3M) and not Drupal-specific, and we lack direct FISMA/ATO lead experience. We have no direct federal API management or CDN/hosting migration at this scale. Our Section 508 and accessibility track record is strong, but not at the "platform-wide, always-on" level CMS expects. Cybersecurity is a real gap.
deep_analysis.win_theme_deep: BLN24 is the only small business prime running a federal Drupal ecosystem at the scale and complexity CMS needs — proven by our MBDA.gov work powering five Commerce Department sites and 60,000+ pages. Combined with our $16M CMS MNPS content operations and our IRS/CBP HCD leadership, we bring a unique blend of Drupal, health data, and user-centered design experience tailored to CMS’s open data mission.
deep_analysis.pp_mapping:
- **Agile Product Management, HCD, User Research:** IRS Human Centered Design BPA ($5.8M) → User research, journey mapping, design systems for IRS digital services; CBP Digital Transformation ($1.4M) → Agile delivery, HCD, user testing
- **Drupal CMS Development & Operations:** MBDA Website Refresh and Sustainment ($4.8M) → Prime on Drupal 9.5, custom module/component dev, multi-tenant architecture, 60,000+ pages, Section 508 compliance, multilingual workflow
- **Section 508/WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance:** MBDA Website Refresh ($4.8M) → 508 audits, remediation, and compliance for Drupal; CMS MNPS ($16M) → 508-compliant content and notices for CMS programs
- **Data Operations (Publishing, QA):** CMS MNPS ($16M) → Content/data publishing, multi-stakeholder QA, regulated content workflows for Medicare/Medicaid
- **Help Desk Operations:** MBDA Website Refresh ($4.8M) → Ongoing Drupal platform support and user help desk for Commerce digital ecosystem
- **DevSecOps, CI/CD:** MBDA Website Refresh ($4.8M) → CI/CD pipelines for Drupal; Census DWS III ($8.3M) → CI/CD for AEM, performance monitoring, but not Drupal-specific
- **API Management:** Census DWS III ($8.3M) → API integration and analytics for AEM, but not Drupal or open data APIs
- **ATO Maintenance, Cybersecurity:** No direct FISMA/ATO lead; Census DWS III ($8.3M) → Supported ATO as sub, not prime; MBDA.gov → Security best practices, but not full ATO
- **Hosting/CDN Migration, Data Website Consolidation:** MBDA Website Refresh ($4.8M) → Multi-site Drupal consolidation, but not hosting/CDN migration at CMS scale
deep_analysis.honest_gaps:
- **ATO/Cybersecurity/FISMA:** No direct experience as prime leading a FISMA ATO for a federal Drupal platform. Mitigation: Partner with a proven ATO shop (e.g., AFS, trusted sub) and highlight security best practices from MBDA.gov and Census DWS III.
- **DevSecOps at CMS Scale:** CI/CD and DevOps experience is strong, but not at the 99.5% uptime, always-on, open data scale CMS expects. Mitigation: Staff with senior DevSecOps/CloudOps leads with direct CMS or HHS experience; propose a phased transition.
- **API Management:** No direct federal open data API management at this scale. Mitigation: Bring in a specialized API partner or hire for this gap; leverage Census DWS III API integration as partial evidence.
- **Hosting/CDN Migration:** No direct evidence of large-scale federal hosting/CDN migration for Drupal. Mitigation: Subcontract this work to a known federal cloud integrator.
deep_analysis.loss_risks:
- Evaluators flag lack of direct FISMA/ATO lead experience for a Drupal platform at this scale
- Weakness in DevSecOps and API management compared to incumbents or large integrators
- Perception that our 508/accessibility experience is content-focused, not platform-wide
- CMS bias toward incumbents or vendors with existing data.CMS.gov or open data platform experience
deep_analysis.watch_notes_deep:
- Immediate: Identify and approach a proven federal ATO/Cybersecurity partner for teaming.
- Assign capture lead to map out potential API/DevSecOps/CloudOps partners with CMS/HHS credentials this week.

opps.title: Decennial Schedule Support (DSS)
opps.description:
PURPOSE:
The purpose of this requirement is for the Government to receive the following: 
-	Support and maintenance of the 2030 Census lifecycle (includes the 2026 Census Test, 2028 Dress Rehearsal, 2030 Execution, and other related tests), and IMS (described above in section 3.2),
-	Closeout and archive 2030 Census lifecycle (2026 Census Test, 2028 Dress Rehearsal, 2030 Execution, and other related tests) IMS data as directed by the USCB,
-	Document Lessons Learned and suggested improvements for future decennial program/project schedules,
-	Assist with the development of the 2040 schedule architecture,
-	Enhancements to the Decennial Directorate Schedule Management System and 2030 Census lifecycle Project Schedules, to allow for greater efficiency of automated processing,
-	Enhancements to the Decennial Directorate Schedule Management System and 2030 Census Project Schedules, to allow for improved data exchange with external partners,
-	Enhancements to improve data quality of the Decennial Directorate Schedule Management System and 2030 Census lifecycle Project Schedules,
-	Implementation of more cost-effective processes for updating the 2030 Census lifecycle IMS and continuing to keep the schedules current,
-	A full range of Schedule Management services in support of the 2030 Census lifecycle IMS Schedules, Schedule Management Support Services for all Decennial Directorate Divisions and other Decennial Directorate initiatives as directed by the USCB, and
-	Research innovate and adapt newest schedule management best practices, technologies, and trends in the industry as directed by the USCB

SCOPE:
The Contractor shall perform tasks in support of establishing, maintaining, and managing the Decennial Schedule System, the DCMD Project Schedules, the Decennial Schedule Management Plan and Processes, and the Decennial Integrated Master Schedule (IMS) as defined by SB for the 2030 Census lifecycle. This includes management of the Oracle Primavera and support of the Proj
opps.summary:
Background
 The US Census Bureau (USCB) is seeking support for the 2030 Census lifecycle, which includes the 2026 Census Test, 2028 Dress Rehearsal, and 2030 Execution. 
The goal of this contract is to provide comprehensive support and maintenance for the Integrated Master Schedule (IMS) associated with these activities, ensuring effective management and documentation of lessons learned for future decennial programs.

Work Details

- Support and maintenance of the 2030 Census lifecycle IMS data as directed by USCB. 
- Closeout and archive of IMS d

opps.title: Decennial Transformation & Application Modernization Acquisition
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework supports the Business Ecosystem, the Decennial (2030 Census and early 2040 Census research), as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, research methodology programs, and other program areas within USCB. The purpose is to plan, develop, document, test, train, monitor, manage, and maintain software architecture and applications to support the 2030 Decennial Census operations. DTAM is the modernization of existing applications and the development of new applications, across Decennial systems, that will be leveraged for the Decennial Census Programs, in support of the 2028 Census Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census.
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Partial cloud/modernization match — Clarity24 JV has NOAA cloud delivery experience
Small business set-aside

tasks.note: BLN24 JV — Clarity24 NOAA app mod $2.8M, Census MAF/TIGER modernization

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: Forecast — early awareness, shape before RFP
$100.0M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Census Bureau's massive digital modernization initiative — the CenTAM framework — covering IT systems for the 2030 Decennial Census. Includes application development, legacy modernization, cloud migration, data collection platforms, and systems to support economic, geographic, and demographic programs. Multiple separate contract awards expected under this framework.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has direct Census past performance ($4.5M MAF/TIGER modernization, $4.5M EDL Applications, $2.8M NOAA app mod). Census is also BLN24's highest-value client ($18.4M). This is a real opportunity — but it's a large technology modernization vehicle, not comms or HCD. The question is which specific task areas under CenTAM align with what Clarity24 can actually deliver vs. what larger primes will own. Full & Open means big integrators will compete.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Get into the weeds on the CenTAM acquisition structure — it's a collection of complementary contracts, not one big award. Find which specific package maps to digital modernization vs. IT infrastructure vs. data engineering. Clarity24 should be positioned for the digital/app mod pieces. Due 2027 — shape now.

opps.title: Census Transformation & Application Modernization (CenTAM) Acquisition Framework
opps.description: The CenTAM Acquisition Framework is designed to support the USCB’s ongoing modernization efforts by integrating data science with traditional survey methods to diversify its data products and place data at the core of USCB operations. This framework will support the Business ecosystem, the Decennial – 2030 Census and early 2040 Census research, as well as IT, economic, geographic, demographic, and research methodology programs.

opps.title: DOC - Managed Printing Services - MRAS
opps.description:
On behalf of Department of Commerce (DOC), GSA is conducting market research.

DO NOT submit your response in eBuy. Your response will not be included in the report that is provided to the customer. NOTE: If the link does not work, try clearing your browser history (cache) and then copying and pasting the link into a new browser. To respond to this RFI, you MUST copy and paste the below link into a new browser.

Only responses submitted through the linked RFI will be accepted. Nothing else will be evaluated or recorded (this includes selecting 'interested' within eBuy).


Link to RFI: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_e2H02UMJasIUPpY


Additional Information:
Due to the structure of contracts in GSA eBuy, you may receive multiple Sources Sought or RFI notifications for the same requirement if it falls under multiple of your company's awarded SINs. However, please note that you are only required to respond to the RFI once.

Be sure to use the URL provided above to submit your response.


Any questions regarding this RFI will be responded to by email only.  Send your inquiry to: rfi@research.gsa.gov and be sure to include the RFQ number.
opps.summary:
Background
 The U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC) is a cabinet-level department that aims to promote job creation, economic growth, sustainable development, and improved standards of living for all Americans. 

The DOC collaborates with businesses, universities, communities, and workers to enhance American business innovation and competitiveness. The DOC consists of 11 Bureaus and the Office of the Secretary, employing over 45,000 individuals globally. Under the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), the DOC seeks to provide Managed Printing Services (MPS) at the Herbert C. Hoover Bui

rex_analysis.what_they_need: NOAA's vehicle for Science, Engineering, and Technical Support Services across its research offices — weather, oceans, fisheries, atmospheric research. Task orders span scientific program support, systems engineering, data management, IT systems, and environmental compliance across ~235 NOAA facilities.
rex_analysis.rex_take: Clarity24 JV has $7M in confirmed NOAA delivery (cloud migration, app modernization), which is real past performance. But SETSS is primarily a science and engineering vehicle — meteorology, oceanography, fisheries, facilities. BLN24's lanes (HCD, comms, data analytics) are narrow within this IDIQ. Best path is monitoring for specific task orders in digital/data/comms scope rather than pursuing the IDIQ prime. Due Jan 2027 — early awareness only.
rex_analysis.watch_notes: Watch for task order RFPs under the existing SETSS vehicle that align with digital, UX, or data analytics scope. Clarity24's NOAA relationship is the asset here. Avoid chasing the IDIQ prime unless a teaming partner with deep NOAA scientific credentials is in place.

opps.title: Technology Test and Evaluation Division (TTED) Technology Evaluations Support Services
opps.agency: National Institute of Standards and Technology
opps.description:
This is a Combined Synopsis / Solicitation for Commercial Items prepared in accordance with the format in FAR Subpart 12.6 – Streamlined Procedures for Evaluation and Solicitation of Commercial Items – as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This solicitation is a Request for Quotations (RFQ). The solicitation document and incorporated provisions and clauses are those in effect through Federal Acquisition Circular 2026-01, effective March 13, 2026.  



The associated North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code for this procurement is 541990 – All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services with a small business size standard of $19.5 Million.



This acquisition is being procured as a 100% small business set-aside; all qualified small business concerns capable of providing the required services are eligible.



FAR Part 12 and acquisition procedures will be utilized.



The Government intends to award one or more Indefinite Delivery/ Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract(s) to provide Technology Evaluations Support Services for the Technology Test and Evaluation Division (TTED) at the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).



The maximum cumulative amount of all orders placed under the awarded IDIQ contract shall be $7,400,000.00.



See attached SF1449 and its attachments for pertinent details of this solicitation.  Please note that this is a draft solicitation and the final solicitation is expected to be issued on or around April 23, 2026.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) is a non-regulatory federal agency within the U.S. Department of Commerce, established to promote U.S. innovation and industrial competitiveness by advancing measurement science, standards, and technology.

The Technology Test and Evaluation Division (TTED) within NIST's Information Technology Laboratory (ITL) conducts evaluation-driven research programs to accelerate the readiness of emerging technologies for deployment. This contract aims to provide Technology Evaluations Support Services to assist TTED in its mission.

Wo

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

competitors.name: Mathematica Policy Research
competitors.why: Premier federal evaluation firm. Wins IES and ED evaluation contracts regularly. They are THE main competitor on any IES program evaluation work — they essentially invented rigorous federal program evaluation. Check their IES-specific awards on HigherGov.
competitors.evidence: IES, ED, HHS — program evaluation, RCT design, education research

competitors.name: Westat
competitors.why: Federal statistical research firm. Survey methodology and program evaluation at Census, ED, and HHS. Regular IES contractor.
competitors.evidence: ED, Census, HHS — surveys, evaluation, statistical research

competitors.name: Abt Associates
competitors.why: Federal research and evaluation firm. Education, health, and workforce program evaluation. Active on IES and ED evaluation contracts.
competitors.evidence: ED, IES, Labor — program evaluation, workforce research

opps.title: Tsunami Warning System Information System Security Officer (ISSO) Services
opps.description:
The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Weather Service (NWS) requires Tsunami Warning System Information System Security Officer (ISSO) services.

All questions must be submitted via the Question Submission Form no later than 2:00 p.m. Pacific Time on April 27, 2026. The Government will only respond to questions and comments submitted through the Google Form. Submit one question or comment on each form. Quoters may make multiple submissions to ask multiple questions.

A two-phase down-select strategy will be used for this procurement. All interested firms are required to submit a Phase 1 quote. Phase 1 quotes must be submitted using the Phase I Submission Form no later than 2:00 PM Pacific Time on May 4, 2026. It is the responsibility of the quoter to ensure the Government received the quote before the stated due date and time. Quoters are strongly encouraged to validate that the Government received its quote. 

A due date for Phase II will be established after the evaluation of Phase I quotes, and will be included in the advisory notification. 

Responses submitted via e-Buy will NOT be accepted.

This contract aims to provide specialized technical expertise in information security compliance, particularly for the National Weather Service (NWS) Tsunami Warning System in the Alaska and Pacific Regions.
